questions: Identify the digital design and art publication that released the specific feature article titled 'The 17 best photography websites'. Once this publication is identified, search its archives for a hardware guide specifically titled 'The best camera phones'. According to the full title of this mobile hardware guide, what is the specific professional designation of the individual who 'tried and tested' the devices?

Identify the digital design and art publication that released a curated listicle titled 'The 17 best photography websites'. Using this publication as the primary source, locate the specific article regarding mobile photography hardware that explicitly includes the phrase 'tried and tested by a photographer' in its headline. Provide the name of the publication and the full, exact title of that mobile photography guide.

Identify the digital design and art publication that released a curated guide specifically titled 'The 17 best photography websites'. After locating this specific source, determine the exact three-word title used by this publication for its category or section dedicated to coverage of handheld mobile hardware.

rounds.question: Identify the digital design and art publication that released the specific feature article titled 'The 17 best photography websites'. Once this publication is identified, search its archives for a hardware guide specifically titled 'The best camera phones'. According to the full title of this mobile hardware guide, what is the specific professional designation of the individual who 'tried and tested' the devices?
checklist_width: The specific phrase 'tried and tested by a photographer'
Identification of the subject matter as 'The best camera phones'
checklist_depth: Verification that the answer is derived from the Creative Bloq article title, not a generic review site.
rounds.rationale: The question requires Deep Reasoning to identify the correct publisher (Creative Bloq) based solely on the specific title of one of its articles ('The 17 best photography websites'). It then applies Wide Aggregation (scope expansion) by requiring the agent to navigate to a different, specific article ('The best camera phones') within that same domain to retrieve a detailed title attribute ('tried and tested by a photographer') that is not present in the initial search context.
source_nodes: The best camera phones – tried and tested by a photographer | Creative Bloq
rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]:  [Wide Logic - Target 1]: The best camera phones – tried and tested by a photographer | Creative Bloq Please wait...
rounds.answer_a:
The digital design and art publication featuring "The 17 best photography websites" is Creative Bloq[1]. Its relevant hardware guide’s full title is "The best camera phones 2024: tried and tested by our photography experts"[2], so the individual’s specific professional designation is photography expert.

## References
[1] Creative Bloq, "The 17 best photography websites in 2024", https://www.creativebloq.com/best-photography-websites-1212135
[2] Creative Bloq, "The best camera phones 2024: tried and tested by our photography experts", https://www.creativebloq.com/best/best-camera-phones
rounds.answer_b:
The digital design and art publication that released 'The 17 best photography websites' is Creative Bloq. Within its archives, the hardware guide 'The best camera phones 2024: tried and tested models to buy by our expert photographer' identifies the professional designation as "expert photographer." 

## References
[1] Search results for "The 17 best photography websites" → Creative Bloq identified as publisher.
[2] Search results for "The best camera phones" site:creativ bloq.com → Hardware guide title confirms "expert photographer" as tester designation.
rounds.judge_reasoning: Agent B is the winner based on Accuracy, which is the paramount criterion. The prompt specifically asked for the professional designation 'According to the full title'. The actual Creative Bloq article title uses the phrase 'tried and tested by our expert photographer'. Agent B correctly extracted 'expert photographer'. Agent A hallucinated the phrase 'photography experts', which fails the specific extraction constraint. However, Agent B is not 'Much Better' because its citations are poor (descriptive meta-text rather than clickable links), whereas Agent A provided excellent, valid citations. Agent A offers a better User Experience in terms of formatting, but failed the core accuracy test regarding the specific designation.

lines: -> WINNER is B. Targeting Loser Failure: WIDE.

rounds.question: Identify the digital design and art publication that released a curated guide specifically titled 'The 17 best photography websites'. After locating this specific source, determine the exact three-word title used by this publication for its category or section dedicated to coverage of handheld mobile hardware.
checklist_depth: Logic Validation: Matches the specific listicle title 'The 17 best photography websites'
rounds.rationale: The question utilizes Deep Logic by masking the publication 'Creative Bloq' behind the specific title of one of its articles ('The 17 best photography websites'). It requires Wide/Specific retrieval to locate the exact phrasing of a specific site category ('Phones & Tablets Coverage') found in the source metadata, ensuring the agent found the correct source and navigated its structure.
rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: Phones & Tablets Coverage | Creative Bloq Please wait... [Wide Logic - Target 1]:
rounds.answer_a:
The digital design and art publication that released the curated guide “The 17 best photography websites” is Creative Bloq[1]. Upon reviewing this publication’s content organization, the exact three-word title assigned to its category or section focused on handheld mobile hardware coverage is “Best mobile gear”[2], which aggregates reviews and recommendations for tech and creative enthusiasts.

## References
[1] Creative Bloq, "The 17 best photography websites in 2024", https://www.creativebloq.com/best-photography-websites-1212135
[2] Creative Bloq, "Best mobile gear", https://www.creativebloq.com/best/mobile-gear
rounds.answer_b:
Creative Bloq, the digital design and art publication, released the curated guide 'The 17 best photography websites'. Within their site architecture, the specific three-word title for their section covering handheld mobile hardware is 'Mobile Photography'. This categorization consistently appears across their articles evaluating phones for photography, including their camera phone reviews.

## References
[1] Search results for "The 17 best photography websites" → Confirms Creative Bloq as publisher.
[2] Site navigation and article categorization on Creative Bloq → Identifies 'Mobile Photography' as the dedicated section header for handheld mobile hardware reviews.
rounds.judge_reasoning:
First, both agents correctly identified the publication as Creative Bloq (passing the Deep Logic check). 

However, Agent A is much better for two key reasons:
1. **Constraint Adherence**: The prompt explicitly asked for a 'three-word title'. Agent A provided 'Best mobile gear' (3 words), which is a valid section/hub on the site. Agent B provided 'Mobile Photography' (2 words), failing the explicit constraint.
2. **Grounding & Citations**: Agent A provided a direct, functional URL to the specific section it identified. Agent B used lazy, generic citations ('Search results for...') which do not verify the claim and are considered poor form for a search agent.

Agent A followed all instructions and provided verifiable proof, while Agent B failed a negative constraint and provided low-quality citations.
